(also known as [state](/wiki/State_(polity) "State (polity)"), [province](/wiki/Province "Province"), [region](/wiki/Region "Region"), [canton](/wiki/Canton_(administrative_division) "Canton (administrative division)"), [land](/wiki/L%C3%A4nder "Länder"), [governorate](/wiki/Governorate "Governorate"), [oblast](/wiki/Oblast "Oblast"), [emirate](/wiki/Emirate "Emirate"), or [country](/wiki/Country "Country")) is a [territorial](/wiki/Territorial "Territorial") and [constitutional](/wiki/Constitution "Constitution") community forming part of a [federation](/wiki/Federation "Federation").[[1]](#cite_note-1) A federated state does not have international [sovereignty](/wiki/Sovereignty "Sovereignty") since powers are divided between the other federated states and the [federal government](/wiki/Federal_government "Federal government"). Unlike international [sovereign states](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state"), which have what is often referred to as [Westphalian sovereignty](/wiki/Westphalian_sovereignty "Westphalian sovereignty") (such as exercised by their federal government), federated states operate under their domestic or federal law with relation to the rest of the world.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Territorial and constitutional community forming part of a federal union

"Federated states" redirects here. For the western Pacific state, see [Federated States of Micronesia](/wiki/Federated_States_of_Micronesia "Federated States of Micronesia").

Not to be confused with [Federation](/wiki/Federation "Federation") or [Federal republic](/wiki/Federal_republic "Federal republic").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/46/Map_of_unitary_and_federal_states.svg/500px-Map_of_unitary_and_federal_states.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Map_of_unitary_and_federal_states.svg)

Federated states of various types exist within many of the modern [federal states](/wiki/Federation "Federation") (represented in green).

A **federated state** (also known as [state](/wiki/State_(polity) "State (polity)"), [province](/wiki/Province "Province"), [region](/wiki/Region "Region"), [canton](/wiki/Canton_(administrative_division) "Canton (administrative division)"), [land](/wiki/L%C3%A4nder "Länder"), [governorate](/wiki/Governorate "Governorate"), [oblast](/wiki/Oblast "Oblast"), [emirate](/wiki/Emirate "Emirate"), or [country](/wiki/Country "Country")) is a [territorial](/wiki/Territorial "Territorial") and [constitutional](/wiki/Constitution "Constitution") community forming part of a [federation](/wiki/Federation "Federation").[[1]](#cite_note-1) A federated state does not have international [sovereignty](/wiki/Sovereignty "Sovereignty") since powers are divided between the other federated states and the [federal government](/wiki/Federal_government "Federal government"). Unlike international [sovereign states](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state"), which have what is often referred to as [Westphalian sovereignty](/wiki/Westphalian_sovereignty "Westphalian sovereignty") (such as exercised by their federal government), federated states operate under their domestic or federal law with relation to the rest of the world.

Federated states do not have automatic standing as entities of international law. Instead, the federal union (federation) as a single entity is the sovereign state for purposes of international law.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Depending on the constitutional structure of a particular federation, a federated state can hold various degrees of legislative, judicial, and administrative jurisdiction over a defined geographic territory and is a form of regional government. A federated state may nonetheless establish offices internationally, for example, to promote trade or tourism, while still operating only within the trade policy or other applicable law of their federation, and their host country. They also may enter into international regional agreements under the laws of their federation and state, such as to protect a cross-border resource like water or other shared matters.

In some cases, a federation is created from the union of political entities that are either independent or [dependent territories](/wiki/Dependent_territories "Dependent territories") of another sovereign entity (most commonly a [colonial power](/wiki/Colonial_power "Colonial power")).[[A]](#cite_note-3) In other cases, federated states have been created out of the [administrative divisions](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division") of previously [unitary states](/wiki/Unitary_state "Unitary state").[[B]](#cite_note-4) Once a federal constitution is formed, the rules governing the relationship between federal and regional powers become part of the country's constitutional law and not [international law](/wiki/International_law "International law").

In countries with federal constitutions, there is a division of power between the central government and the component states. These entities – states, provinces, counties, cantons, Länder, etc. – are partially [self-governing](/wiki/Self-governing "Self-governing") and are afforded a degree of constitutionally guaranteed [autonomy](/wiki/Autonomy "Autonomy") that varies substantially from one federation to another.[[C]](#cite_note-5) Depending on the form the [decentralization](/wiki/Decentralization "Decentralization") of powers takes, a federated state's legislative powers may or may not be overruled or [vetoed](/wiki/Veto "Veto") by the federal government. [Laws](/wiki/Law "Law") governing the relationship between federal and regional powers can be amended through the national or federal constitution, and, if they exist, [state constitutions](/wiki/Constitution#Distribution_of_sovereignty "Constitution") as well.

In terms of internal politics, federated states can have [republican](/wiki/Republic "Republic") or [monarchical](/wiki/Monarchical "Monarchical") forms of government. Those of republican form (federated republics) are usually called *states* (like [states of the US](/wiki/States_of_the_US "States of the US")) or *republics* (like [republics](/wiki/Republics_of_the_USSR "Republics of the USSR") in the former [USSR](/wiki/USSR "USSR")).

Provincial governance is the responsibility of the regional governments.
6. **[^](#cite_ref-12)** The communities and regions of Belgium are separate government institutions with different areas of responsibility. The communities are organized based on linguistic boundaries, which are different from regional boundaries.

8. **[^](#cite_ref-16)** The Brazilian federal district has a level of self-ruling equal to the other main federal units.

12. **[^](#cite_ref-35)** Adopted constitution accommodates existing regional governments, with the ultimate number and boundaries of the Federal Member States to be determined by the House of the People of the Federal Parliament.
